exposures.observed_outcome: peak reached ~192 at 16:28 local, required 20u correction — leaning high for Day 1 at elevated pre-meal glucose
22u on Day 2 — appears better matched than the 29u on Day 1 at similar carbs

notes: Best-characterised food in the log. 10.5g per 250ml serving is well-established. Two clean exposures on different Mounjaro days. Day 1 29u for 2 servings + correction still ran high; Day 2 22u appeared better. Carb estimate is reliable — dose variation is Mounjaro day and pre-meal glucose effect.

exposures.observed_outcome: 4u at glucose 117 — glucose rose to 133 within 1h, required 4u manual correction. Slight underdose or dawn-effect timing.
4u again, outcome not separately tracked — subsequent boluses make it hard to isolate
conservative dose on downward trend — not a clean calibration point

notes: Label carbs reliable. 4u for 14g carbs at target glucose on Day 1 with Rc=1.0 implies ICR working as expected but may need small correction component added if pre-meal glucose is elevated.

exposures.observed_outcome: no bolus given — glucose behaviour not isolated
7u for full charcuterie plate including 12oz gazpacho — carb estimate for gazpacho portion alone is ~5g, rest is meat/cheese. Not isolated.

notes: 5g per 120ml is a reasonable estimate for standard gazpacho (tomato, cucumber, pepper, oil, bread base). Carb confidence medium — homemade recipes vary. Has never caused a significant glucose event as a standalone item. Typically consumed as accompaniment with no dedicated bolus.

exposures.observed_outcome: 20u for croissant + 2 large strawberries (logged as 45g total). Glucose trended up 114->116->123 post-dose — appeared adequately covered. No correction required.

notes: Logged as part of a 45g combined entry with strawberries (~8g). Croissant alone estimated at ~28-35g depending on size. Single clean exposure on Day 0 — well covered at 20u for the full 45g meal.

exposures.observed_outcome: 25u for 200g beans with broth and crème fraiche. Glucose rose to 159 by 16:25, required 3u correction with 8.54u IOB still active. Running somewhat high — beans may be underestimated or absorption slower than modeled.
3 bowls bean soup with Rioja — 30u then 15u correction required. Larger portion, confounded by alcohol and multiple corrections.

notes: Beans are slow-absorbing — glucose rise comes later than expected (60-90 min). 30g for 200g cooked is a reasonable estimate but the Day 3 exposure suggests it may run slightly high. Worth watching on future clean exposures.

exposures.observed_outcome: no dedicated bolus logged — consumed after strawberries/kiwi, glucose trajectory not isolatable
deliberate underdose (10u vs calculated 24u) at Day 6 Rc=1.45. Glucose rose to 202 requiring 20u correction. Clear underdose — model calculation was directionally correct.

notes: Label carbs reliable. Day 6 underdose experiment confirms model was right at ~24u for that glucose/Rc combination. Ice cream fat content slows absorption — peak often delayed 60-90 min. Do not underdose at high Rc days.

exposures.observed_outcome: conservative 20u dose. Glucose peaked at 182 by 01:21 requiring 24u correction. Significant underdose — either carbs underestimated or absorption faster than expected at Day 4.

notes: Single exposure suggesting 20u was too conservative for 30g at Day 4. The subsequent 24u correction implies actual coverage need was considerably higher. Popcorn is medium-high GI. Carb estimate may be low or portion was larger than logged.

exposures.observed_outcome: only 14u available in pen at time of meal (glucose 89). Deferred full correction — resulted in hypo from activity-induced sensitivity increase. Not a clean calibration point.

notes: 62g is a rough estimate — IKEA hotdog bun ~25g, fries ~35g. Single exposure completely confounded by wrong-pen situation and subsequent hypo. Carb confidence low. Needs a clean future exposure. Note: eat-first rule applies at pre-meal glucose this low regardless.

exposures.observed_outcome: only 15u given for 78g — heavy underdose, likely intentional given alcohol context. Followed by late-night hypo support eating. Not calibration-quality.

notes: Carb estimate for nachos alone is unreliable — portion size, toppings, and chip quantity vary significantly. Two large beers add ~26g. Alcohol confounds glucose response independently. Needs a clean future exposure without alcohol to characterize.

serving_desc: half of 14-inch thin cracker crust pie

exposures.observed_outcome: 20u at glucose 192 with active IOB from IKEA/hypo treatment sequence earlier. Heavily confounded — not calibration-quality.

notes: 43g for half of a 14-inch thin crust is a reasonable estimate (full pie ~85g). Cracker crust is lower carb than deep dish. Single exposure completely confounded by prior hypo treatment and active IOB. Needs clean future exposure.

serving_desc: 2 medium corn tortilla enchiladas + 330ml beer + 6 tortilla chips with queso

exposures.observed_outcome: split dose 15u then 20u due to low pre-meal glucose (106) and Day 6 resistance. Glucose reached 231 by 08:44 next morning — appears adequate coverage overnight but confounded by Day 6 effects and the full correction cascade that day.

notes: 57g estimate for this combination is plausible — corn tortillas ~15g each, beer ~13g, chips ~8g. Confounded by Day 6 Rc and preceding day's correction cascade. Eat-first rule applied correctly given pre-meal glucose 106.

food: smash burger with fries and beer
serving_desc: single smash burger + fries + 400ml beer (restaurant, Malaga)

exposures.observed_outcome: 30u initial, 30u correction top-up, 20u third dose — 80u total across 3 hours. Glucose peaked ~209 mid-sequence. Third dose likely excessive as carbs were absorbing. Chaotic dosing, unreliable calibration.

notes: Carb estimate of 75g is uncertain — smash burger bun ~30g, fries ~30g, beer ~13g. Possibly overestimated. The 80u total dose strongly suggests either carb overestimate or the multi-dose stacking was the real problem. Next visit: ask for nutrition info, start conservative, watch CGM before topping up.

exposures.observed_outcome: 145g carbs logged but likely a data entry error — 21u for 145g would be a massive underdose. Glucose flattened at 165. Carb figure is suspect.
24u for 160g gyoza at glucose 95, Day 3. Outcome not recorded in log — no subsequent readings linked. Note: eat-first rule should apply at glucose 95.

notes: 160g portion ~32g carbs is a reasonable estimate (gyoza wrappers ~3-4g each, 8-10 pieces). April 13 exposure at glucose 95 — should have triggered eat-first rule. 24u at Day 3 Rc=1.135 seems plausible. Need outcome data from CGM for this exposure.

exposures.observed_outcome: 27u for 21g KitKat + correction from glucose 151. Large correction component dominated. Glucose trajectory post-dose not isolatable from the dinner that followed shortly after.

notes: Label carbs reliable — 27g per standard bar (logged as 21g which may reflect a smaller bar or partial consumption). Single exposure confounded by large correction component and subsequent meal. KitKat is fast-absorbing (high sugar, thin wafer).

exposures.observed_outcome: no bolus given across multiple beers during the afternoon — glucose stayed in acceptable range suggesting alcohol-mediated insulin sensitisation offsetting the carbs

notes: 13g per 330ml is reasonable for standard lager. Alcohol independently lowers glucose via hepatic glucose suppression — in practice beer often needs no dedicated bolus unless glucose is already elevated. Multiple beers with no bolus across Apr 2 afternoon was well-tolerated.

exposures.observed_outcome: hypo treatment at glucose 74. Rapid absorption expected. Glucose rebounded — magnitude not tracked cleanly due to subsequent pizza meal.

notes: Fast-acting hypo treatment. 40g is approximate. Rule: no insulin for hypo treatment carbs — these are intentionally fast-absorbing to raise glucose. Do not apply ICR to hypo treatment entries.
